planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 now focuses the exp87 no-fall visible branch on knee flexion, contact, slip, and terminal return as one joint objective.

perspectives.product: tests whether the remaining M19 gaps can be reduced before paying for another PPO finetune
perspectives.architecture: keeps exp86/87's three-phase WBC-lite controller and changes only the candidate schedule family plus score weights
perspectives.security: no credentials or external side effects
perspectives.qa: native sweep records raw JSON, visible gate metrics, and joint optimizer score per schedule
perspectives.skeptic: knee flexion pressure may trade directly against stance anchoring or terminal stand height

dod: raw native JSON per joint schedule variant
summary ranks schedules and states whether any variant passes visible_8cm_gate

sources.note: G1 lower-body degrees of freedom, joint range, and knee torque make squat geometry plausible.
ASAP reports agile Unitree G1 whole-body skills including squat and squat plus lean, but uses learned motion tracking and sim-to-real alignment.
Whole-body control work motivates unified locomotion/control rather than raw pose tracking for fine-grained humanoid motion.
G1 getting-up work motivates refining discovered motions into deployable transitions under additional constraints.
